items.title: GMN4AD: Graph Matching Network for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis with Test-Time Domain Adaptation using Multi-centered Structure Magnetic Resonance Imaging
items.abstract: This paper introduces GMN4AD, a Graph Matching Network designed to improve Alzheimer's Disease diagnosis using structural MRI by explicitly modeling interactions between heterogeneous brain graphs. The core method leverages graph matching to capture crucial cross-graph relationships, overcoming limitations of independent graph analysis. A key contribution is the integration of a test-time domain adaptation strategy to enhance robustness against multi-centered site heterogeneity.

items.title: Trimodal Glioma Representation Alignment via Volumetric Contrastive Learning
items.abstract: This paper introduces GLORIA, a novel trimodal framework that aligns information from histopathology, mRNA expression, and MRI for glioma prognosis. It uses modality-specific encoders and a Gramian contrastive loss to project these heterogeneous data into a shared latent space. The aligned representations are then fused via a cross-modal gating module for joint optimization of glioma grading and survival prediction.

items.title: Expert-Driven Survival Machines: Improving Stratification and Interpretability in Multiple Clinical Cohorts
items.abstract: This paper introduces AdaCSM, a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) framework designed to improve survival prediction by modeling heterogeneous patient data across multiple clinical cohorts. The core method uses a routing-based expert mechanism to dynamically assign patients to specialized risk predictors, allowing for individualized feature representations rather than a single common one. This approach enhances risk stratification accuracy and provides greater interpretability by explicitly clustering patients into specialized survival subtypes.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1 . AdaCSM pipeline. The model uses a top‑K routing mechanism to assign each input to a subset of experts, which are subsequently processed by a Weibull‑based Deep Clustering Survival Machine. Expert outputs are combined through mixture weights produced by the router, enabling adaptive modeling of heterogeneous risk patterns.

items.title: Towards In Silico Cancer Therapy Design: An Agent-Based Approach for GPU-Accelerated Molecular Pathway Simulation
items.abstract: This paper introduces a GPU-accelerated agent-based simulator for modeling complex molecular pathways in cancer therapy design. The core method represents individual molecules as autonomous agents interacting spatially within a 3D environment, leveraging FLAME GPU 2 for efficient simulation of millions of agents. The contribution is an efficient computational tool capable of analyzing signaling pathways and evaluating therapeutic interventions *in silico*.
items.figure_caption: The simulator interface during a running simulation. The left panel displays the 3D visualisation of the simulation environment, highlighting molecules by compartment (orange for cytoplasm, yellow for nucleus). The right panel shows the real-time concentration plot of cytoplasmic phosphorylated cFos (pcFosc), a downstream product of the MAPK/ERK signalling pathway, reflecting the accumulation and phosphorylation of cFos until a steady-state level is reached. This example, based on Nakakuki et al. [ undefh ] , is discussed in Section 3.4 and demonstrates the simulator’s ability to reproduce gene expression dynamics and compartmental processes. It also highlights the capability to perform simulations in a fully three-dimensional environment without requiring spatial slicing.

items.title: Not everyone is using AI for everything
items.summary: Despite widespread awareness, many people are only using AI tools for specific, limited tasks rather than integrating them into every aspect of their digital lives.

items.title: Rio de Janeiro's "homegrown" LLM appears to be a merge of an existing model
items.summary: A "homegrown" large language model developed in Rio de Janeiro appears to be a merge of an existing, publicly available model, according to an issue raised on its GitHub repository.

items.title: Linux 7.1
items.summary: The Linux 7.1 kernel has been released, bringing updates and new features to the open-source operating system.

items.title: Did Anthropic ask for this?
items.summary: The tech news story likely discusses whether Anthropic, the AI company, was responsible for or requested a specific event or development related to AI.

items.title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations
items.summary: KPMG withdrew a report about AI usage after discovering it contained apparent factual errors or "hallucinations" generated by the artificial intelligence.

items.description: Collection of publicly available IPTV channels from all over the world
items.summary: The `iptv-org/iptv` repository serves as a comprehensive, community-maintained collection of publicly accessible IPTV channel lists from around the globe.

items.description: freeCodeCamp.org's open-source codebase and curriculum. Learn math, programming, and computer science for free.
items.summary: The freeCodeCamp repository provides the open-source codebase and comprehensive curriculum for learning math, programming, and computer science entirely for free.

items.description: The pytest framework makes it easy to write small tests, yet scales to support complex functional testing
items.summary: Pytest is a powerful, scalable testing framework for Python that simplifies writing small tests while supporting complex functional testing needs.

SWC is a super-fast Rust-based platform for the Web, primarily functioning as a next-generation compiler and bundler for JavaScript/TypeScript, offering features like fast compilation, minification, and code transformation.

items.description: Open-source live-chat, email support, omni-channel desk. An alternative to Intercom, Zendesk, Salesforce Service Cloud etc. 🔥💬
items.summary: Chatwoot is an open-source, omni-channel customer support platform offering live chat and email capabilities as a direct alternative to proprietary solutions like Intercom and Zendesk.

items.description: Security scanner for AI agent skills. Detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks.
items.summary: SkillSpector is a security scanner for AI agent skills, designed to detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks within those capabilities.
Meshery is a cloud-native manager that provides a unified platform for designing, deploying, managing, and observing service meshes and cloud-native infrastructure across various environments.

items.description: Fast, easy and reliable testing for anything that runs in a browser.
items.summary: Cypress is a next-generation front-end testing tool that provides a fast, easy, and reliable way to test anything running in a browser.

items.description: 100+ open-source clones of popular sites like Airbnb, Amazon, Instagram, Netflix, Tiktok, Spotify, Whatsapp, Youtube etc. See source code, demo links, tech stack, github stars.
items.summary: This repository provides over 100 open-source clones of popular websites like Airbnb and Netflix, detailing their source code, demo links, technology stacks, and GitHub star counts.
